question:
Identify and retrieve the governing requirements and definitions for Washington State wastewater facility planning and design relevant to AKART and feasibility plan or Engineering Report (G1) expectations. Focus on: 

- Required contents of an Engineering Report (e.g., G1)
- Definitions and use of design flows (ADWF, PDWF, peaking factors; any recommended percentile choices)
- Any Ecology/WA guidance that informs AKART compliance and design criteria
- Cross-references to WAC 173-240-050 / -060 / -070 and Ecology "AKART" guidance

The goal is to surface the correct sections/pages across WAC, Ecology guidance, and the `design_standards` corpus, not to compute numbers.

citations.section: WAC 173-240-070 Plans and specifications. (1) The plans and specifications for a domestic wastewater facility are the detailed construction documents by which the owner or his or her contractor bid and construct the facility. original.summary: These sources were selected to span the full lifecycle of wastewater system planning and design: regulatory submittal requirements (WAC 173-240), state design standards (DOE Orange Book), federal evaluation guidance (EPA), and industry design practice (WEF FD-5). The WAC pages (pp.1–7) establish upstream legal requirements for facility plans and engineering reports, while the Orange Book (pp.5, 45) bridges into design criteria. EPA documents (SSES and I/I, pp.3, 12, 20) provide downstream diagnostic and compliance methods for infiltration/inflow and condition assessment. WEF FD-5 (p.44) supplies detailed gravity sewer design practices that implement the standards in real designs.
rationale_bullets: WAC_173-240_Wastewater_Submission_Plans.pdf (pp.1–7: [2], [5], [1], [3], [8], [7]) defines scope, submittal content, and regulatory thresholds that anchor all planning and report requirements (upstream context).
DOE_Orange_Book_Design_Standards.pdf (p.5: [4]) provides overarching state design principles that translate regulatory intent into engineering criteria.
DOE_Orange_Book_Design_Standards.pdf (p.45: [9]) offers detailed sewer design parameters, linking planning mandates to practical design choices (midstream bridge).
EPA_Sanitary_Sewer_Evaluation_9100WGB6.pdf (pp.3, 20: [10], [12]) outlines SSES program elements and methods, guiding downstream field investigations and compliance verification.
EPA_Infiltration_Inflow_Review.pdf (p.12: [6]) supplies federal guidance on I/I analysis, thresholds, and corrective action planning (downstream performance assessment).

2013_WEF_FD-5_gravity-sanitary-sewer-design-and-construction.pdf (p.44: [11]) provides industry best practices for gravity sewer design that operationalize the standards and support remediation designs.
